title: centaurea × kleineana
intro: Centaurea × kleineana, also known as Kleina's knapweed, is a hybrid plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is a cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea montana. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating a variety of health issues. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting digestive health. Centaurea × kleineana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
